answer:
The electric dipole moment \( p \) is calculated as the product of the charge magnitude \( q \) and the separation distance \( d \):

\[
p = q \times d = (1.50 \times 10^{-9} \, \mathrm{C}) \times (6.20 \times 10^{-6} \, \mathrm{m}) = 9.30 \times 10^{-15} \, \mathrm{C \cdot m}
\]

The correct answer is \(\boxed{I}\).
